allies.rationale_text: Incentives helps prisoners-dilemma reveal when individually rational behavior is being produced by a payoff structure that rewards defection.
allies.source_quote: The model helps separate bad actors from bad rules by making the payoff structure explicit.

allies.rationale_text: Systems-thinking helps prisoners-dilemma diagnose bad collective outcomes as the product of a flawed interaction structure rather than isolated bad intent.
allies.source_quote: It serves as a diagnostic tool in systems thinking, helping users recognize archetypes of common system structures that produce characteristic patterns of behavior.

antagonists.rationale_text: Confirmation-bias worsens prisoners-dilemma dynamics when ambiguous moves are read as confirming that the other side is defecting.
antagonists.source_quote: prisoners-dilemma conflicts with confirmation-bias (confirmation bias) when players interpret ambiguous moves to confirm that the other party is defecting

structured_tensions.tension_text: Prisoners-dilemma conflicts with active-listening when incentive structures reward concealment over transparent coordination.
structured_tensions.source_quote: prisoners-dilemma conflicts with active-listening (active listening) when incentive structures reward concealment over transparent coordination.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Prisoners-dilemma conflicts with empathy when defensive payoff framing overrides perspective-taking and trust repair.
structured_tensions.source_quote: prisoners-dilemma conflicts with empathy (empathy) when defensive payoff framing overrides perspective-taking and trust repair.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Prisoners-dilemma conflicts with confirmation-bias when players interpret ambiguous moves to confirm that the other party is defecting, accelerating mutual defection even when cooperation was intended.
structured_tensions.source_quote: prisoners-dilemma vs confirmation-bias: prisoners-dilemma conflicts with confirmation-bias (confirmation bias) when players interpret ambiguous moves to confirm that the other party is defecting, accelerating mutual defection even when cooperation was intended.

curation_notes.summary: The raw source is strongest on payoff-structure diagnosis, systems archetype framing, and explicit current-vocabulary tensions against active-listening, empathy, and confirmation-bias.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor game-theory relation sprawl beyond the clean incentives, systems, and explicit tension surface.
